messages.content:
1_ Translate the following legal text into colloquial Farsi 2_ Place the Persian and English text side by side in the table 3_ From the beginning to the end of the text, there should be an English sentence on the left side and a Persian sentence on the right side.
       4- Using legal language for Persian translation

          .5
Administrative
tribunals
In his book Freedom Under the Law, Lord Denning refers to administrative tribunals as
'a separate set of courts dealing with a set of rights and duties. Just as in the old days there were ecclesiastical courts dealing with matrimonial cases and the administration of estates, and just as there was the Chancellor dealing with the enforcement and administration of trusts, so in our day there are the new tribunals dealing with the rights and duties between man and the State.'
In the last fifty years successive governments have been concerned with regulating the social life of the community. Legislation for improving the gen- eral well-being has included the National Health Service Act, National Insurance Act, Education Act, Housing Acts, Town and Country Planning Acts, and Rent Restriction Acts. Although the collective good and welfare are admirable aims, the effect of much legislation is that, while promoting the public interest, it also circumscribes the rights of the private individual. There are now over 2,000 such tribunals which hear over 200,000 cases a year.
For example, Adams owns a field which the local Education Authority pro- poses to purchase compulsorily as a site for a technical college. Adams may wish to retain this field and to contest the right of the Education Authority to acquire it. If the field is compulsorily acquired, Adams may further dispute the amount payable to compensate him for his loss. Tribunals may decide (a) whether the field will be acquired, and (b) the amount of compensation.
Similarly, Black is injured at work which disables him from continuing his employment. He can claim a pension under Social Security legislation, but his claim may be repudiated by the Adjudication Officer on the ground that it is not within the Act.
Many such disputes occur between a private individual seeking to protect their own private rights and a Minister, Government department, local authority or other person to whom authority has been given by law to administer a particular Act.

| English | Farsi |
|---------|-------|
| Administrative tribunals | دیوان‌های اداری |
| In his book Freedom Under the Law, Lord Denning refers to administrative tribunals as 'a separate set of courts dealing with a set of rights and duties. | در کتاب آزادی تحت قانون، لرد دنینگ به دیوان‌های اداری به عنوان "مجموعه‌ای جدا از دادگاه‌ها که با مجموعه‌ای از حقوق و وظایف سروکار دارند" اشاره می‌کند. |
| Just as in the old days there were ecclesiastical courts dealing with matrimonial cases and the administration of estates, and just as there was the Chancellor dealing with the enforcement and administration of trusts, so in our day there are the new tribunals dealing with the rights and duties between man and the State.' | همانطور که در روزگاران قدیم دادگاه‌های کلیسایی با موارد ازدواج و مدیریت اموال، و همانطور که مستشار با اجرا و مدیریت امانات سروکار داشت، امروزه دیوان‌های جدیدی وجود دارند که با حقوق و وظایف میان انسان و دولت سروکار دارند. |
| In the last fifty years successive governments have been concerned with regulating the social life of the community. | در پنجاه سال گذشته، دولت‌های متوالی به تنظیم زندگی اجتماعی جامعه علاقمند بوده‌اند. |
| Legislation for improving the general well-being has included the National Health Service Act, National Insurance Act, Education Act, Housing Acts, Town and Country Planning Acts, and Rent Restriction Acts. | قوانین برای بهبود رفاه عمومی شامل قانون خدمات بهداشت ملی، قانون بیمه ملی، قانون آموزش و پرورش، قوانین مسکن، قوانین برنامه‌ریزی شهری و روستایی، و قوانین محدودیت اجاره بوده‌اند. |
| Although the collective good and welfare are admirable aims, the effect of much legislation is that, while promoting the public interest, it also circumscribes the rights of the private individual. | هرچند که خیر جمعی و رفاه اهداف ستودنی هستند، تأثیر بسیاری از قانون‌گذاری‌ها به گونه‌ای است که، در حالی که منافع عمومی را ترویج می‌کنند، همزمان حقوق فرد خصوصی را نیز محدود می‌کنند. |
| There are now over 2,000 such tribunals which hear over 200,000 cases a year. | اکنون بیش از 2,000 چنین دیوانی وجود دارد که بیش از 200,000 پرونده در سال را بررسی می‌کنند. |
| For example, Adams owns a field which the local Education Authority proposes to purchase compulsorily as a site for a technical college. | به عنوان مثال، آدامز مالک یک زمین است که اداره آموزش محلی پیشنهاد خرید اجباری آن را به عنوان مکانی برای دانشکده فنی دارد. |
| Adams may wish to retain this field and to contest the right of the Education Authority to acquire it. | آدامز ممکن است بخواهد این زمین را نگه دارد و حق اداره آموزش برای تصاحب آن را به چالش بکشد. |
| If the field is compulsorily acquired, Adams may further dispute the amount payable to compensate him for his loss. | اگر زمین به طور اجباری تصاحب شود، آدامز ممکن است مبلغ قابل پرداخت برای جبران خسارت خود را نیز مورد اختلاف قرار دهد. |
| Tribunals may decide (a) whether the field will be acquired, and (b) the amount of compensation. | دیوان‌ها ممکن است تصمیم بگیرند (الف) آیا زمین تصاحب خواهد شد، و (ب) میزان غرامت. |
| Similarly, Black is injured at work which disables him from continuing his employment. | به طور مشابه، بلک در کار آسیب می‌بیند که او را از ادامه کار ناتوان می‌سازد. |
| He can claim a pension under Social Security legislation, but his claim may be repudiated by the Adjudication Officer on the ground that it is not within the Act. | او می‌تواند تحت قانونگذاری امنیت اجتماعی بازنشستگی درخواست کند، اما ادعای او توسط مامور ارزیابی به این دلیل که در چارچوب قانون نیست، رد شده ممکن است. |
| Many such disputes occur between a private individual seeking to protect their own private rights and a Minister, Government department, local authority or other person to whom authority has been given by law to administer a particular Act. | بسیاری از چنین اختلاف‌هایی بین یک فرد خصوصی که در جستجوی حفاظت از حقوق خصوصی خود است و یک وزیر، دپارتمان دولتی، مقامات محلی یا شخص دیگری که به وسیله قانون اختیار مدیریت قانون خاصی را دارند، رخ می‌دهد. |

It may be argued that disputes of this kind ought to be decided in the
traditionally impartial and fair atmosphere of a court of law which follows a
known procedure and applies a known system of law - common law or
statute. But we find that such disputes are frequently decided by special tri-
bunals, not on the basis of law, but on grounds of policy and discretion, and
that as far as possible the tribunals endeavour to reconcile the rights of the
individual with those of the public in whose general interest the particular
legislation may have been passed.
The position occupied by administrative tribunals and the type of law
applied therein, known as administrative law, is a matter of great import-
ance. At this stage, therefore, we shall examine some of the reasons advanced
for their creation, and the advantages and disadvantages advantages which
administrative tribunals display.
The reasons usually given for the establishment of administrative
tribunals are:
(a) Ordinary courts are already overburdened with work, and additional
jurisdiction would cause a breakdown.
(b) The costs of judicial proceedings in ordinary courts would be heavy.
CO
The courts of law are slow and the procedure elaborate.
Matters involving a public service are best administered by specialists in
that service, e.g. doctors at a medical appeal.
(e) Policy decisions are best settled by an administrative authority.

| English | Farsi |
|---------|-------|
| It may be argued that disputes of this kind ought to be decided in the traditionally impartial and fair atmosphere of a court of law which follows a known procedure and applies a known system of law - common law or statute. | ممکن است استدلال شود که اختلافات از این دست باید در فضای بی‌طرف و عادلانه‌ی سنتی یک دادگاه قانونی که از یک رویه معلوم پیروی کرده و یک سیستم قانونی شناخته شده - قانون عرفی یا مقررات - را اعمال می‌کند، حل و فصل شوند. |
| But we find that such disputes are frequently decided by special tribunals, not on the basis of law, but on grounds of policy and discretion, and that as far as possible the tribunals endeavour to reconcile the rights of the individual with those of the public in whose general interest the particular legislation may have been passed. | اما ما می‌یابیم که چنین اختلاف‌هایی معمولاً توسط دیوان‌های خاص حل و فصل می‌شوند، نه بر اساس قانون، بلکه بر اساس سیاست و اختیار، و تا جایی که ممکن است دیوان‌ها تلاش می‌کنند حقوق فرد را با حقوق عموم که قانون خاص برای منافع عمومی تصویب شده باشد، آشتی دهند. |
| The position occupied by administrative tribunals and the type of law applied therein, known as administrative law, is a matter of great importance. | موقعیتی که توسط دیوان‌های اداری اشغال شده و نوع قانونی که در آن‌ها اعمال می‌شود، که به عنوان حقوق اداری شناخته می‌شود، موضوعی از اهمیت زیاد است. |
| At this stage, therefore, we shall examine some of the reasons advanced for their creation, and the advantages and disadvantages which administrative tribunals display. | بنابراین، در این مرحله، ما برخی از دلایل پیشرفته برای ایجاد آن‌ها و مزایا و معایبی که دیوان‌های اداری نشان می‌دهند را بررسی خواهیم کرد. |
| The reasons usually given for the establishment of administrative tribunals are: | دلایلی که معمولاً برای ایجاد دیوان‌های اداری داده می‌شوند، عبارتند از: |
| (a) Ordinary courts are already overburdened with work, and additional jurisdiction would cause a breakdown. | (الف) دادگاه‌های عادی هم‌اکنون با کار بیش از حد مواجه هستند و قضاوت اضافی باعث فروپاشی خواهد شد. |
| (b) The costs of judicial proceedings in ordinary courts would be heavy. | (ب) هزینه‌های رسیدگی قضایی در دادگاه‌های عادی سنگین خواهد بود. |
| The courts of law are slow and the procedure elaborate. | دادگاه‌های قانونی کند هستند و رویه بسیار پیچیده است. |
| Matters involving a public service are best administered by specialists in that service, e.g. doctors at a medical appeal. | امور مربوط به یک خدمت عمومی بهتر است توسط متخصصان آن خدمت، مثلاً پزشکان در یک تجدید نظر پزشکی، اداره شوند. |
| (e) Policy decisions are best settled by an administrative authority. | (ه) تصمیمات سیاستی بهترین است که توسط یک مقام اداری حل و فصل شوند. |

The advantages of administrative tribunals are said to be:
Decisions are quick and delays are avoided.
(ii) The procedure is cheap; usually no fees are payable.
The informal atmosphere and straightforward procedure suit the litigant. (iv) They have wide discretionary power. This avoids the rigidity which the doctrine of precedent imposes on the courts of law.
(v) Tribunals are often staffed by experts: e.g. doctors on Pensions Tribunals determine disability or extent of injury.
(vi) Tribunals ensure efficient administration of social or economic policies found in statutes; while courts of law sift facts and decide on the basis of established rules of law different in character from social policy.
The disadvantages are said to be:
(i) Administrative tribunals are sometimes held in private and lack publicity. Suspicion may be aroused as to ‘administrative justice'. (ii) The parties are sometimes prohibited from being represented by lawyers. The inarticulate person is therefore at a disadvantage in explaining his or her case. Legal aid is not generally available (see p. 65). (iii) Reasons for decisions are not always published.
(iv) Technical experts and administrators are not always capable of acting impartially or of sifting the facts.
(v) Tribunals sometimes include a civil servant of the Ministry which is directly involved in the dispute. He is, therefore, not sufficiently inde- pendent or impartial to give a just decision.
(vi) Rights of appeal are limited in some cases.
(vii) Discretion of a tribunal is sometimes so wide as to make decisions inconsistent and unpredictable.

| English | Farsi |
|---------|-------|
| The advantages of administrative tribunals are said to be: | گفته می‌شود مزایای دیوان‌های اداری عبارتند از: |
| Decisions are quick and delays are avoided. | تصمیمات سریع هستند و از تأخیر اجتناب می‌شود. |
| (ii) The procedure is cheap; usually no fees are payable. | (دوم) رویه ارزان است؛ معمولاً هیچ هزینه‌ای پرداخت نمی‌شود. |
| The informal atmosphere and straightforward procedure suit the litigant. | فضای غیررسمی و رویه‌ی روشن و مستقیم، به شاکی می‌آید. |
| (iv) They have wide discretionary power. This avoids the rigidity which the doctrine of precedent imposes on the courts of law. | (چهارم) آن‌ها قدرت اختیار وسیعی دارند. این امر از سخت‌گیری‌هایی که دکترین سابقه بر دادگاه‌های قانونی تحمیل می‌کند، جلوگیری می‌کند. |
| (v) Tribunals are often staffed by experts: e.g. doctors on Pensions Tribunals determine disability or extent of injury. | (پنجم) دیوان‌ها اغلب توسط متخصصان تشکیل می‌شوند: مثلاً پزشکان در دیوان‌های بازنشستگی میزان ناتوانی یا شدت آسیب را تعیین می‌کنند. |
| (vi) Tribunals ensure efficient administration of social or economic policies found in statutes; while courts of law sift facts and decide on the basis of established rules of law different in character from social policy. | (ششم) دیوان‌ها مدیریت مؤثر سیاست‌های اجتماعی یا اقتصادی یافت شده در قوانین را اطمینان می‌دهند؛ در حالی که دادگاه‌های قانون واقعیات را الک می‌کنند و بر اساس قوانین مستقری که از نظر شخصیت از سیاست‌های اجتماعی متفاوت هستند تصمیم می‌گیرند. |
| The disadvantages are said to be: | گفته می‌شود معایب عبارتند از: |
| (i) Administrative tribunals are sometimes held in private and lack publicity. Suspicion may be aroused as to ‘administrative justice'. | (اول) دیوان‌های اداری گاهی به صورت خصوصی برگزار شده و از شهرت کافی برخوردار نیستند. شک و تردید نسبت به 'عدالت اداری' ممکن است برانگیخته شود. |
| (ii) The parties are sometimes prohibited from being represented by lawyers. The inarticulate person is therefore at a disadvantage in explaining his or her case. Legal aid is not generally available. | (دوم) گاهی طرف‌ها از نمایندگی توسط وکلا منع می‌شوند. بنابراین، فردی که قادر به بیان درست نیست، در شرح مورد خود دچار معایب می‌شود. کمک‌های حقوقی به طور کلی در دسترس نیست. |
| (iii) Reasons for decisions are not always published. | (سوم) دلایل تصمیمات همیشه منتشر نمی‌شوند. |
| (iv) Technical experts and administrators are not always capable of acting impartially or of sifting the facts. | (چهارم) متخصصان فنی و مدیران همیشه قادر به عمل کردن بی‌طرفانه یا الک کردن حقایق نیستند. |
| (v) Tribunals sometimes include a civil servant of the Ministry which is directly involved in the dispute. He is, therefore, not sufficiently independent or impartial to give a just decision. | (پنجم) گاهی اوقات دیوان‌ها شامل یک کارمند دولتی از وزارتخانه‌ای می‌شوند که مستقیماً در اختلاف دخیل است. بنابراین، وی به اندازه کافی مستقل یا بی‌طرف برای دادن یک تصمیم عادلانه نیست. |
| (vi) Rights of appeal are limited in some cases. | (ششم) حقوق تجدیدنظر در برخی موارد محدود است. |
| (vii) Discretion of a tribunal is sometimes so wide as to make decisions inconsistent and unpredictable. | (هفتم) اختیار یک دیوان گاهی به قدری گسترده است که تصمیمات ناسازگار و غیرقابل پیش‌بینی می‌شود. |

Examples of tribunals
Social Security. Claims for benefits under the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act, 1992, are dealt with locally by a Social Security officer. If disagreement arises the applicant for benefit has a right of appeal to a local tribunal made up of a chairman, who is a lawyer, and two lay members, onerepresenting employers' organizations and one representing employees' organizations. From the tribunal's decision appeal lies to the Social Security Commissioner, a barrister appointed by the Crown.
There is no right of appeal either on a point of law or fact from the Commissioner's finding. Some technical questions are reserved for decision by the Minister, who may refer the matter to a judge of the High Court. From the Minister's decision on such questions there is a right of appeal to the High Court (whose decision is final).
Industrial Injuries. There is a similar system of adjudication in respect of industrial injuries claims under the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act, 1992. The insured person must establish that the injury arose during the course of their employment. If the insurance officer disallows the claim the applicant may appeal to the tribunal applicable to Social Security (see above).
If the applicant establishes that the injury did arise in the course of employment, the next question to consider is the extent of the disablement. This claim is decided first by a medical board of two doctors. Further appeal lies to a medical appeal tribunal made up of two doctors with a lawyer as chairman. From the medical appeal tribunal appeal lies to the Social Security Commissioner.
Decisions of the Commissioner in Social Security and industrial injuries cases are published officially, and such decisions bind insurance officers and local tribunals.

| English | Farsi |
|---------|-------|
| Examples of tribunals | نمونه‌هایی از دیوان‌ها |
| Social Security. Claims for benefits under the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act, 1992, are dealt with locally by a Social Security officer. | امنیت اجتماعی. ادعاها برای مزایا تحت قانون مشارکت‌ها و مزایای امنیت اجتماعی، 1992، به صورت محلی توسط یک افسر امنیت اجتماعی مورد بررسی قرار می‌گیرد. |
| If disagreement arises the applicant for benefit has a right of appeal to a local tribunal made up of a chairman, who is a lawyer, and two lay members, one representing employers' organizations and one representing employees' organizations. | اگر اختلاف نظری پیش بیاید، متقاضی مزایا حق دارد به یک دیوان محلی که متشکل از یک رئیس، که وکیل است، و دو عضو غیرمتخصص، یکی نماینده سازمان‌های کارفرمایان و دیگری نماینده سازمان‌های کارگران، اعتراض کند. |
| From the tribunal's decision appeal lies to the Social Security Commissioner, a barrister appointed by the Crown. | از تصمیم دیوان، اعتراض به کمیسر امنیت اجتماعی، وکیلی که توسط تاج منصوب شده، وجود دارد. |
| There is no right of appeal either on a point of law or fact from the Commissioner's finding. | هیچ حق اعتراضی، چه بر اساس نقطه‌ای از قانون یا واقعیت، از یافته‌های کمیسر وجود ندارد. |
| Some technical questions are reserved for decision by the Minister, who may refer the matter to a judge of the High Court. | برخی سوالات فنی برای تصمیم‌گیری توسط وزیر محفوظ است، که ممکن است موضوع را به قاضی دادگاه عالی ارجاع دهد. |
| From the Minister's decision on such questions there is a right of appeal to the High Court (whose decision is final). | از تصمیم وزیر درباره چنین سوالاتی، حق اعتراض به دادگاه عالی (که تصمیم آن نهایی است) وجود دارد. |
| Industrial Injuries. There is a similar system of adjudication in respect of industrial injuries claims under the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act, 1992. | صدمات صنعتی. سیستم مشابهی از قضاوت در مورد ادعاهای صدمات صنعتی تحت قانون مشارکت‌ها و مزایای امنیت اجتماعی، 1992، وجود دارد. |
| The insured person must establish that the injury arose during the course of their employment. | فرد بیمه شده باید اثبات کند که آسیب در جریان اشتغال به کار او رخ داده است. |
| If the insurance officer disallows the claim the applicant may appeal to the tribunal applicable to Social Security (see above). | اگر افسر بیمه ادعا را نپذیرد، متقاضی می‌تواند به دیوان مربوط به امنیت اجتماعی (به بالا مراجعه کنید) اعتراض کند. |
| If the applicant establishes that the injury did arise in the course of employment, the next question to consider is the extent of the disablement. | اگر متقاضی اثبات کند که آسیب در جریان اشتغال به کار اتفاق افتاده است، سوال بعدی که باید در نظر گرفته شود، میزان ناتوانی است. |
| This claim is decided first by a medical board of two doctors. | این ادعا ابتدا توسط یک هیئت پزشکی متشکل از دو دکتر تصمیم گیری می‌شود. |
| Further appeal lies to a medical appeal tribunal made up of two doctors with a lawyer as chairman. | اعتراض بیشتر به یک دیوان تجدیدنظر پزشکی که متشکل از دو دکتر با یک وکیل به عنوان رئیس می‌باشد، وجود دارد. |
| From the medical appeal tribunal appeal lies to the Social Security Commissioner. | از دیوان تجدیدنظر پزشکی، اعتراض به کمیسر امنیت اجتماعی وجود دارد. |
| Decisions of the Commissioner in Social Security and industrial injuries cases are published officially, and such decisions bind insurance officers and local tribunals. | تصمیمات کمیسر در موارد امنیت اجتماعی و صدمات صنعتی به طور رسمی منتشر می‌شوند، و چنین تصمیماتی افسران بیمه و دیوان‌های محلی را ملزم می‌کند. |

Employment Tribunals (formerly Industrial Tribunals). The Industrial Training Act, 1964 (replaced by the Industrial Training Act, 1982), provided for the establishment of these tribunals. Later legislation has considerably increased their jurisdiction, particularly the Trade Union and Labour Relations (Consolidation) Act, 1992, and the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act, 1978. Most of the cases brought to the tribunals con- cern unfair dismissal or redundancy. The Industrial Tribunals Act, 1996 con- solidated various enactments relating to industrial tribunals and the Employment Appeal Tribunal. In addition to these areas the tribunals have jurisdiction over many aspects of industrial law. Thousands of cases are referred to the tribunals, which sit throughout the country.
In accordance with the Employment Rights (Dispute Resolutions) Act, 1998 Industrial Tribunals were re-named Employment Tribunals with the title 'President of the Employment Tribunals (England and Wales)' replacing 'President of the Industrial Tribunals (England and Wales)'.
The principal aim of the 1998 Act was to improve access to justice in employment rights disputes by streamlining procedures before employment tribunals and by encouraging greater use of alternative methods of dispute resolution. The Advisory, Conciliation and Arbitration Service (ACAS) is empowered, subject to the Secretary of State's approval, to prepare, fund and promote an arbitrator's scheme for the resolution of unfair dismissal disputes. The tribunal consists of a legally qualified chairman and two lay members (where the parties agree, the Chairman may sit with only one lay member). The applicant may be legally represented or may be represented by a trade-union official; he or she may send written representations setting out the facts and arguments; they may require the production of documents, and may request the tribunal to order the attendance of any person to give evi- dence and to produce documents.The decision of the tribunal, which is by majority, is given in writing with reasons therefore. The decision is subject to a right of appeal to the Employment Appeal Tribunal on a point of law.

| English | Farsi |
|---------|-------|
| Employment Tribunals (formerly Industrial Tribunals). | دیوان‌های کار (پیشتر دیوان‌های صنعتی). |
| The Industrial Training Act, 1964 (replaced by the Industrial Training Act, 1982), provided for the establishment of these tribunals. | قانون آموزش صنعتی، 1964 (که توسط قانون آموزش صنعتی، 1982، جایگزین شد)، برای تأسیس این دیوان‌ها در نظر گرفته شده بود. |
| Later legislation has considerably increased their jurisdiction, particularly the Trade Union and Labour Relations (Consolidation) Act, 1992, and the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act, 1978. | قوانین بعدی، دامنه صلاحیت آن‌ها را به طور قابل توجهی افزایش داد، به ویژه قانون اتحادیه‌های تجاری و روابط کار (ادغام)، 1992، و قانون حفاظت از اشتغال (ادغام)، 1978. |
| Most of the cases brought to the tribunals concern unfair dismissal or redundancy. | بیشتر پرونده‌هایی که به دیوان‌ها آورده می‌شوند، مربوط به اخراج‌های ناعادلانه یا اضافی بودن نیروی کار هستند. |
| The Industrial Tribunals Act, 1996 consolidated various enactments relating to industrial tribunals and the Employment Appeal Tribunal. | قانون دیوان‌های صنعتی، 1996، قوانین مختلف مرتبط با دیوان‌های صنعتی و دیوان تجدیدنظر اشتغال را ادغام کرد. |
| In addition to these areas the tribunals have jurisdiction over many aspects of industrial law. | علاوه بر این حوزه‌ها، دیوان‌ها در بسیاری از جنبه‌های قانون صنعتی صلاحیت دارند. |
| Thousands of cases are referred to the tribunals, which sit throughout the country. | هزاران پرونده به دیوان‌ها ارجاع داده می‌شوند که در سراسر کشور تشکیل جلسه می‌دهند. |
| In accordance with the Employment Rights (Dispute Resolutions) Act, 1998 Industrial Tribunals were re-named Employment Tribunals with the title 'President of the Employment Tribunals (England and Wales)' replacing 'President of the Industrial Tribunals (England and Wales)'. | مطابق با قانون حقوق اشتغال (حل اختلاف)، 1998، دیوان‌های صنعتی به دیوان‌های کار با عنوان 'رئیس دیوان‌های کار (انگلستان و ولز)' تغییر نام داده شدند که جایگزین 'رئیس دیوان‌های صنعتی (انگلستان و ولز)' شد. |
| The principal aim of the 1998 Act was to improve access to justice in employment rights disputes by streamlining procedures before employment tribunals and by encouraging greater use of alternative methods of dispute resolution. | هدف اصلی قانون 1998 افزایش دسترسی به عدالت در اختلافات حقوق کار با تسهیل رویه‌ها قبل از دیوان‌های کار و تشویق به استفاده بیشتر از روش‌های جایگزین حل اختلاف بود. |
| The Advisory, Conciliation and Arbitration Service (ACAS) is empowered, subject to the Secretary of State's approval, to prepare, fund and promote an arbitrator's scheme for the resolution of unfair dismissal disputes. | خدمات مشاوره، آشتی و داوری (ACAS)، مشروط بر تأیید وزیر دولت، مجاز است برای حل اختلافات اخراج ناعادلانه، طرحی داوری را آماده، تأمین مالی و ترویج کند. |
| The tribunal consists of a legally qualified chairman and two lay members (where the parties agree, the Chairman may sit with only one lay member). | دیوان از یک رئیس با صلاحیت قانونی و دو عضو غیرمتخصص تشکیل شده است (در صورت توافق طرف‌ها، رئیس ممکن است تنها با یک عضو غیرمتخصص جلسه بگذارد). |
| The applicant may be legally represented or may be represented by a trade-union official; he or she may send written representations setting out the facts and arguments; they may require the production of documents, and may request the tribunal to order the attendance of any person to give evidence and to produce documents. | متقاضی می‌تواند توسط وکیل یا مقام رسمی اتحادیه کارگری نمایندگی شود؛ وی می‌تواند اظهارات کتبی‌ای که واقعیات و استدلال‌ها را بیان می‌کند، ارسال کند؛ ممکن است تولید اسناد را درخواست کنند، و می‌توانند از دیوان درخواست کنند تا حضور هر فردی برای ارائه شهادت و تولید اسناد را دستور دهد. |
| The decision of the tribunal, which is by majority, is given in writing with reasons therefore. | تصمیم دیوان، که توسط اکثریت اتخاذ می‌شود، به صورت کتبی با دلایل آن ارائه می‌شود. |
| The decision is subject to a right of appeal to the Employment Appeal Tribunal on a point of law. | تصمیم به حق اعتراض به دیوان تجدیدنظر اشتغال در نقطه‌ای از قانون مشمول است. |

Rent Tribunals. Under the Rent Acts the tenant of furnished accommoda- tion may apply to a rent tribunal for the rent to be reduced. Similarly, the tenant of unfurnished accommodation below a certain rateable value may apply to a rent officer to fix a fair rent. Appeal from that decision lies to a rent assessment committee, which normally consists of a legally qualified chairman, a valuer or surveyor and a lay person.
Legal representation is allowed, hearings are open to the public and the Press, and evidence is never taken on oath. If requested, reasons for the deci- sions of the rent assessment committee may be given in writing or orally. Appeal, on a point of law only, lies to the High Court.

| English | Farsi |
|---------|-------|
| Rent Tribunals. | دیوان‌های اجاره. |
| Under the Rent Acts the tenant of furnished accommodation may apply to a rent tribunal for the rent to be reduced. | تحت قوانین اجاره، مستأجر مسکن مبله می‌تواند به دیوان اجاره مراجعه کند تا اجاره‌بها کاهش یابد. |
| Similarly, the tenant of unfurnished accommodation below a certain rateable value may apply to a rent officer to fix a fair rent. | به همین ترتیب، مستأجر مسکن بدون مبله زیر مقدار معین قابل ارزیابی می‌تواند به افسر اجاره مراجعه کند تا اجاره‌بهای منصفانه‌ای تعیین شود. |
| Appeal from that decision lies to a rent assessment committee, which normally consists of a legally qualified chairman, a valuer or surveyor and a lay person. | اعتراض از آن تصمیم به کمیته ارزیابی اجاره برده می‌شود که معمولاً از یک رئیس با صلاحیت قانونی، یک ارزیاب یا نقشه‌بردار و یک شخص غیرمتخصص تشکیل شده است. |
| Legal representation is allowed, hearings are open to the public and the Press, and evidence is never taken on oath. | نمایندگی قانونی مجاز است، جلسات شنیدنی برای عموم و رسانه‌ها باز است و هرگز شهادت‌ها تحت سوگند اخذ نمی‌شوند. |
| If requested, reasons for the decisions of the rent assessment committee may be given in writing or orally. | در صورت درخواست، دلایل تصمیمات کمیته ارزیابی اجاره می‌تواند به صورت کتبی یا شفاهی ارائه شود. |
| Appeal, on a point of law only, lies to the High Court. | اعتراض، تنها بر اساس نقطه‌ای از قانون، به دادگاه عالی برده می‌شود. |

Domestic Tribunals. Domestic tribunals exist to determine questions, decide disputes and maintain discipline among members of a particular trade or profession. Thus a trade union or a professional body may lay down its set of rules governing membership. If the rules are infringed a tribunal may be set up to deal with the incident and it may punish or expel the offender. Three important examples of domestic tribunals are:
(a) Trade Unions. The disciplinary tribunals of trade unions are created by members themselves. A governing committee frames the rules of mem- bership, rules constituting a tribunal, rules of procedure and the forms of punishment. Members who violate the rules may be expelled or fined by the disciplinary tribunal. The Trade Union and Labour Relations (Consolidation) Act, 1992, altered the law in regard to trade unions. Trade unions maintain certain disciplinary powers in regard to members, but where a wrong is alleged to be done to a member they have a right of appeal to an industrial tribunal, e.g. where he or she has been wrongfully expelled or refused the right to take part in the activities of a trade union. Employees are protected from dismissal for belonging to a union or taking part in its activities. This protection extends to members of all independent unions.
(b) Solicitors. The Solicitors Act, 1974, as amended by the Administration of Justice Act, 1982, provides for the setting up of a committee to exercise disciplinary powers over solicitors. The disciplinary committee sits as a board with a minimum of three members, and follows the usual legal procedure of a court of law. The board may strike a solicitor off the roll, suspend him or her from practice, impose a fine of up to £3,000, or order the payment of costs. Appeal from the committee lies to the High Court.
(c) Doctors. The professional conduct committee of the General Medical Council has power under the Medical Act, 1983, to strike a doctor off the Medical Register for infamous conduct in his or her profession, and he or she can be barred from further practice. The Medical (Professional Performance) Act, 1995 sets out the rules governing the suspension or elimination of the person's name from the register. Appeal against the decision of the General Medical Council lies to the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council.
The General Dental Council has similar powers under the Dentists Act, 1984, concerning dentists; and the Central Midwives Board has similar authority over midwives.

The National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act, 2002, established a Council for the Regulation of Health Care Professionals. The intention of the legislation is to create a more uniform system for each of the professional health care bodies.
Another function is to provide an oversight of the self-regulatory func- tions conferred on each professional regulatory body such as doctors, den- tists, opticians, osteopaths, and chiropractitioners.
There are many more such tribunals, but lack of space precludes their description.

|---------|-------|

If the rules are infringed a tribunal may be set up to deal with the incident and it may punish or expel the offender. | بنابراین، یک اتحادیه تجاری یا یک نهاد حرفه‌ای ممکن است مجموعه قوانین حاکم بر عضویت را تعیین کند. اگر قوانین نقض شوند ممکن است یک دیوان برای رسیدگی به ماجرا تشکیل شود و ممکن است مجرم را مجازات یا اخراج کند. |

The professional conduct committee of the General Medical Council has power under the Medical Act, 1983, to strike a doctor off the Medical Register for infamous conduct in his or her profession. | (ج) پزشکان. کمیته رفتار حرفه‌ای شورای پزشکی عمومی تحت قانون پزشکی، 1983، قدرت دارد یک پزشک را به دلیل رفتار ناشایست در حرفه‌اش از سجل پزشکی حذف کند. |
| The National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act, 2002, established a Council for the Regulation of Health Care Professionals. | قانون اصلاح خدمات بهداشت ملی و حرفه‌های مراقبت های بهداشتی، 2002، یک شورای برای نظارت بر حرفه‌های بهداشتی و مراقبتی را تاسیس کرد. |
| The intention of the legislation is to create a more uniform system for each of the professional health care bodies. | قصد از این قانونگذاری ایجاد یک سیستم یکپارچه‌تر برای هر یک از نهادهای حرفه‌ای مراقبت‌های بهداشتی است. |
| Another function is to provide an oversight of the self-regulatory functions conferred on each professional regulatory body such as doctors, dentists, opticians, osteopaths, and chiropractitioners. | یک عملکرد دیگر ارائه نظارت بر عملکردهای خودتنظیمی است که بر هر نهاد نظارتی حرفه‌ای مانند پزشکان، دندانپزشکان، بینایی‌سنج‌ها، استئوپات‌ها، و کایروپرکتورها اعطا شده است. |
| There are many more such tribunals, but lack of space precludes their description. | دیوان‌های بسیار بیشتری از این دست وجود دارند، اما کمبود فضا مانع از توضیح آن‌ها می‌شود. |

6 Judicial control of administrative
tribunals
A tribunal is another name for a court, but to avoid confusion the term is applied to those bodies possessing judicial powers which operate outside the traditional courts of law. The essential purpose of a tribunal is to adjudicate in a dispute, to follow proper procedures, to act fairly and impartially, and finally to reach a decision. We have seen that many of the tribunals have very wide powers indeed; therefore their judgments must be subject to the super- visory control of the courts of law. Such control is exercised mainly by the Queen's Bench Division which continues the ancient jurisdiction of the ori- ginal royal court of King's Bench.
As a rule Parliament leaves the professional organizations to form their own rules and procedures to maintain control of their membership. Only in excep- tional cases will the courts of law interfere with these. But where the administra- tion of a statute, the exercise of powers under it, and the setting up of tribunals to decide disputes are entrusted to a Minister, Parliament generally requires that certain rules be framed to ensure fairness in the administration of the statute and of tribunals set up thereunder. Nevertheless injustices do occur, and the purpose of this section is to examine the grounds on which the courts of law exercise their supervisory jurisdiction, and the procedures that are followed.
Where a tribunal acts judicially it must follow certain unwritten rules of com- mon law known as ‘natural justice'. Natural justice embraces two sub-rules: (a) The rule against bias ('no man may be a judge in his own cause'); and (b) Audi alteram partem ('hear the other side').

|---------|-------|

| The essential purpose of a tribunal is to adjudicate in a dispute, to follow proper procedures, to act fairly and impartially, and finally to reach a decision. | هدف اصلی یک دیوان این است که در یک اختلاف قضاوت کند، رویه‌های صحیح را پیگیری کند، به شکلی عادلانه و بی‌طرفانه عمل کند، و در نهایت به یک تصمیم برسد. |
| We have seen that many of the tribunals have very wide powers indeed; therefore their judgments must be subject to the supervisory control of the courts of law. | ما دیده‌ایم که بسیاری از دیوان‌ها واقعاً قدرت‌های بسیار گسترده‌ای دارند؛ بنابراین حکم‌های آن‌ها باید تحت کنترل نظارتی دادگاه‌های قانونی باشد. |
| Such control is exercised mainly by the Queen's Bench Division which continues the ancient jurisdiction of the original royal court of King's Bench. | چنین کنترلی عمدتاً توسط بخش نیمکت ملکه اعمال می‌شود که صلاحیت دیرین دادگاه سلطنتی اصلی نیمکت پادشاه را ادامه می‌دهد. |

| But where the administration of a statute, the exercise of powers under it, and the setting up of tribunals to decide disputes are entrusted to a Minister, Parliament generally requires that certain rules be framed to ensure fairness in the administration of the statute and of tribunals set up thereunder. | اما جایی که اداره یک مقررات، اعمال قدرت‌های تحت آن، و تشکیل دیوان‌ها برای حل و فصل اختلافات به وزیر واگذار شده، پارلمان معمولاً نیاز دارد که قوانین خاصی برای تضمین عدالت در اداره مقررات و دیوان‌های تشکیل شده تحت آن، طراحی شود. |
| Nevertheless injustices do occur, and the purpose of this section is to examine the grounds on which the courts of law exercise their supervisory jurisdiction, and the procedures that are followed. | با این حال بی‌عدالتی‌ها رخ می‌دهد، و هدف این بخش بررسی دلایلی است که دادگاه‌های قانونی صلاحیت نظارتی خود را اعمال می‌کنند، و رویه‌هایی که دنبال می‌شود. |
| Where a tribunal acts judicially it must follow certain unwritten rules of common law known as ‘natural justice'. | جایی که یک دیوان به طور قضایی عمل می‌کند، باید قوانین نانوشته حقوق عرفی معروف به 'عدالت طبیعی' را دنبال کند. |
| Natural justice embraces two sub-rules: (a) The rule against bias ('no man may be a judge in his own cause'); and (b) Audi alteram partem ('hear the other side'). | عدالت طبیعی دو زیرقانون را در برمی‌گیرد: (الف) قانون در مقابل تعصب ('هیچ کس نمی‌تواند در مورد خود قاضی باشد'); و (ب) آئودی آلترام پارتم ('طرف دیگر را شنیدن'). |

(a) The rule against bias
A true judicial decision can be reached only if the judge is impartial. This is an obvious requirement in a court of law or a tribunal, and is equally applicable to any other public body with power to affect an individual's rights. In R. v. Rand (1866) it was held that a judge is disqualified where he or she has a direct pecuniary interest, however small, in the subject-matter in dispute; or (ii) there is real likelihood that the judge would have a bias in favour of one of the parties. For example, if a judge is related to, or is a friend of, one of the parties to a dispute there would be real likelihood of bias. It is immaterial whether a judicial decision was in fact biased, for as was said by Lord Chief Justice Hewart in R. v. Sussex Justices, ex parte McCarthy (1924): Justice should not only be done, but should manifestly and undoubtedly be seen to be done.' As an example of pecuniary bias we may quote:
Dimes v. Grand Junction Canal (1852)
Lord Chancellor Cottenham made decrees in a Chancery suit in favour of a canal company. Lord Cottenham held several shares in the company. Held (by the House of Lords): that the decrees be set aside on the ground of pecuniary interest. No bias was proved in fact, nor could it be shown that Lord Cottenham was in any way influenced by his shareholding.

| English | Farsi |
|---------|-------|
| (a) The rule against bias | (الف) قانون در مقابل تعصب |
| A true judicial decision can be reached only if the judge is impartial. | یک تصمیم قضایی واقعی تنها زمانی می‌تواند اتخاذ شود که قاضی بی‌طرف باشد. |
| This is an obvious requirement in a court of law or a tribunal, and is equally applicable to any other public body with power to affect an individual's rights. | این یک نیاز آشکار در یک دادگاه یا دیوان است، و به طور یکسان قابل اعمال بر هر نهاد عمومی دیگری با قدرت تاثیر بر حقوق فردی است. |
| In R. v. Rand (1866) it was held that a judge is disqualified where he or she has a direct pecuniary interest, however small, in the subject-matter in dispute; or (ii) there is real likelihood that the judge would have a bias in favour of one of the parties. | در رأی R. v. Rand (1866) تصمیم گرفته شد که قاضی زمانی غیر واجد شرایط است که وی علاقهٔ مالی مستقیم، هرچند کوچک، در موضوع مورد اختلاف داشته باشد؛ یا (دوم) احتمال واقعی وجود دارد که قاضی تعصبی به نفع یکی از طرف‌ها داشته باشد. |
| For example, if a judge is related to, or is a friend of, one of the parties to a dispute there would be real likelihood of bias. | برای مثال، اگر قاضی به یکی از طرف‌های دعوا وابستگی خانوادگی داشته باشد، یا دوست یکی از آن‌ها باشد، احتمال واقعی تعصب وجود دارد. |
| It is immaterial whether a judicial decision was in fact biased, for as was said by Lord Chief Justice Hewart in R. v. Sussex Justices, ex parte McCarthy (1924): Justice should not only be done, but should manifestly and undoubtedly be seen to be done. | اینکه یک تصمیم قضایی در واقع متعصبانه بوده یا نه مهم نیست، زیرا همانطور که لرد چیف جاستیس هیوارت در R. v. Sussex Justices, ex parte McCarthy (1924) گفت: عدالت نه تنها باید انجام شود، بلکه کاملا و بدون شک باید دیده شود که در حال انجام است. |
| As an example of pecuniary bias we may quote: Dimes v. Grand Junction Canal (1852) Lord Chancellor Cottenham made decrees in a Chancery suit in favour of a canal company. Lord Cottenham held several shares in the company. Held (by the House of Lords): that the decrees be set aside on the ground of pecuniary interest. | به عنوان مثالی از تعصب مالی می‌توانیم به مورد Dimes v. Grand Junction Canal (1852) اشاره کنیم. لرد کنسلر کوتنهام حکم‌هایی را در یک دعوای حقوقی به نفع یک شرکت کانال صادر کرد. لرد کوتنهام چندین سهم در شرکت داشت. تصمیم گرفته شد (توسط مجلس اعیان): که حکم‌ها به دلیل علاقهٔ مالی کنار گذاشته شوند. |
| No bias was proved in fact, nor could it be shown that Lord Cottenham was in any way influenced by his shareholding. | هیچ تعصبی در واقع اثبات نشد، همچنین نمی‌توانست نشان داده شود که لرد کوتنهام به هر شکلی تحت تاثیر سهامداری خود بود. |

As an example of likelihood of bias we may quote:
R. v. Sussex Justices, ex parte McCarthy (1924) A was summoned before magistrates for a motoring offence. The acting clerk to the justices was a member of a firm of solicitors representing A in civil proceed- ings arising out of the same accident. The acting clerk did not advise the magis- trates, but he retired with them to consider their decision. Held: that as the acting clerk was connected with the case in the civil action he ought not to advise the magistrates in the criminal prosecution. Conviction accordingly quashed, despite the fact that the acting clerk took no part in the decision to convict and had not been asked by the justices to give his opinion or advice.

| A was summoned before magistrates for a motoring offence. The acting clerk to the justices was a member of a firm of solicitors representing A in civil proceedings arising out of the same accident. | فردی به نام A برای یک تخلف رانندگی پیش قضات فراخوانده شد. دبیر جاری دادگاه عضوی از یک دفتر وکلایی بود که A را در روند قضایی مدنی ناشی از همان تصادف نمایندگی می‌کرد. |

(b) 'Audi alteram partem'
The second rule of natural justice is that a person has the right to be heard in their own defence. It is contrary to the spirit of our laws that anyone should be convicted without having an opportunity of being heard in their own defence' (R. v. Benn and Church, 1795).
The rule embraces the propositions that the party sued or prosecuted should have the opportunity to:
(i) know the case against them; and (ii) state their case (orally or in writing);
Ridge v. Baldwin (1964)
The Chief Constable of Brighton was dismissed from the force by the watch committee. The Chief Constable was not present at the meeting of the Committee nor was he given an opportunity of stating his case. The Court of Appeal held in favour of the defendants. Ridge appealed to the House of Lords which overruled the Court of Appeal. Held: that, first, there were disciplinary regulations (Police Regs.) which laid down the procedure to be followed in dis- missals; and, further, natural justice required that a hearing should have been given before the watch committee exercised its power. The failure to give a hear- ing as required by natural justice invalidated the dismissal.
A further rule is no doubt that parties have a right to be informed of the reason for the decision. The courts of law follow strict procedural rules which have been hammered out over the centuries to ensure fairness and are also guided by the provisions of the European Convention on Human Rights. Administrative tribunals, however, are not bound to follow these rules meticu- lously but must apply the general principles of justice as shown above.

Supervision by the
Queen's Bench Division
We have mentioned earlier that the monarch is 'the fountain of justice' and that one part of the jurisdiction of the Queen's Bench Division is supervisory. Thus the Queen's Bench Division, acting for the Crown, supervises the administration of justice by inferior courts, administrative tribunals and other tribunals throughout the kingdom. Moreover the Queen's Bench Division exercises supervisory control over Ministers of the Crown, civil servants, local authorities or other authorities purporting to exercise statutory powers. If such authorities act ultra vires (i.e. beyond the powers conferred on them
The courts today 63
by statute), the Queen's Bench Division declare such excess of power to
be void.
may
The control exercised by the Queen's Bench Division is by means of prerogative orders of (a) mandamus, (b) prohibition, and (c) certiorari. Before the Administration of Justice (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act, 1938, these were known as prerogative writs.
(a) Mandamus is an order issuing out of the Queen's Bench Division commanding (mandamus; we command) a person or body to perform a duty imposed by common law or statute. The order is available to enforce administrative duties, e.g. to compel a local authority to produce its accounts for inspection by a ratepayer; or judicial duties, e.g. to com- pel a housing tribunal to hear and determine an appeal, or magistrates to decide a case in petty sessions.
(b) Prohibition is an order issuing out of the Queen's Bench Division to prohibit an inferior court or tribunal from continuing to exceed, or threatening to exceed, its jurisdiction. Thus the order may be directed to recorders, magistrates, coroners, and all statutory tribunals.
(c) Certiorari is an order removing the decision of an inferior judicial body into the Queen's Bench Division to have its legality inquired into. The word certiorari means ‘to be informed'. The order may be used (i) to secure an impartial trial; (ii) to review an excess of jurisdiction; (iii) to challenge an ultra vires act; (iv) to quash a judicial decision made con- trary to natural justice; and (v) to correct errors of law on the face of the record.
An order of certiorari will lie 'wherever any body of person having legal authority to determine questions affecting the rights of subjects, and having the duty to act judicially, act in excess of their legal authority' (Lord Atkin in R. v. Electricity Commissioners, 1924). Thus it can be issued to magistrates' courts, administrative tribunals, disciplinary tri- bunals of the police and fire service, and to arbitrators.
Applications for review were made under one of these prerogative writs, and the problem was that if the applicant claimed under the wrong writ, the whole application could fail.
In 1977 major changes were made to R.S.C. Order 53, to provide a new form of procedure known as 'application for judicial review' which enables an applicant to seek any one or more of the remedies: certiorari, prohibition mandamus, injunction, declaration or damages. This means that more than one remedy can be sought and remedies can be claimed in the alternative.
